column1: One row = one coded practice event. Start small, keep labels consistent, and expand over years 2–3.

column14: Realised I was framing the work too much around interface and not enough around participation.
The conversation exposed that my question was still too broad, which helped narrow the next experiment.
I was overcommitting to form before I had a clear rationale for why this interaction mattered.
Peer feedback pushed me to test process documentation itself as material, not just support evidence.
Building a rough visualisation made the research feel more situated in my own practice rather than borrowed methods.
The review made me ask whether I was merely documenting process or actually exposing how my own choices shaped it.
